title: Creation of RuntimeInterface type to facilitate unit testing
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Currently AFAIK, it's fairly difficult to unit test code that depends on Runtime. The creation of an interface like [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8266/files#diff-0577c1c9b036a03143b3ce475228a67d592abbe0cfe5a07379c150c6f3d385f6R52) and having things like [`Volume` depend on the interface instead](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8266/files#diff-7c030c9b2c05e750a4297fec08a4b4c09372a340bfd741591d7a30cf8dd387c4) would facilitate the create of a Mock for unit testing.

Before I go further down that rabbit hole, is this desired? My personal goal is to build out the `podman play` API because I want that for personal use. This would make it easier for me to write unit tests.

Cons,
* Need to expose more internal objects like `lockManager`
* Maintain a Mock in addition to the main code (I personally think there could be substantial reuse once built out a bit more)
